patch.value: GH-623: opt-in post-merge history reconciliation hook and one-command verify repair

GitHub issue #623 reports that a successful git merge can leave history verification red until a separate manual history-repair pass; provide an explicit safe reconciliation workflow without weakening verification.

An opt-in post-merge hook or one-command reconcile path detects merged history drift, previews the affected streams, performs audited field-aware repair, and leaves pm history --verify plus pm validate green; default git hooks remain non-surprising; docs and sandboxed merge acceptance tests cover enable, skip, failure, and recovery paths.

value.author: codex-20260720
value.text: Duplicate check 2026-07-20: pm search across all statuses for GH-623 and merge history verify red/manual history-repair found no live item; closed pm-g5sx and pm-wc1r provide the shipped merge-driver lineage, while this item captures the newly reported opt-in post-merge orchestration gap. Source: https://github.com/unbraind/pm-cli/issues/623
